description: The session management module generates tokens with only 32 bits of entropy using a predictable PRNG seeded with system time. An attacker can brute-force valid tokens and hijack authenticated sessions without credentials. Demo record 1.
mitigation: Upgrade com.fake:demo-session-manager to 3.0.0 or replace token generation with java.security.SecureRandom with at least 128-bit entropy.
title: Broken Authentication: Session tokens with insufficient entropy - Demo 1

description: The user search endpoint concatenates user-supplied input directly into SQL statements without sanitization. An unauthenticated attacker can extract the full database contents or execute arbitrary DDL commands. Demo record 2.
mitigation: Upgrade io.example:demo-auth-lib to 5.1.3 and replace all dynamic query construction with prepared statements.
title: SQL Injection via unparameterized dynamic query builder - Demo 2

description: AES-128 encryption keys are hardcoded in the application source code and committed to the version control history. Any developer or CI/CD agent with repository access can retrieve and misuse these keys. Demo record 3.
mitigation: Rotate all exposed keys immediately. Move secrets to a vault solution (e.g. HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager). Remove key material from git history.
title: Sensitive Data Exposure: Cryptographic keys stored in source code - Demo 3

description: Error messages rendered by the XML parser module reflect unescaped request parameters into the HTML response. A remote attacker can craft a malicious link and execute arbitrary JavaScript in the victim's browser context. Demo record 4.
mitigation: Upgrade org.demo:fake-xml-parser to 1.1.0 and apply output encoding on all user-supplied values before HTML rendering.
title: Cross-Site Scripting (Reflected) in error response body - Demo 4

description: The logger library is configured to write full exception stack traces and internal class paths into HTTP responses in production. This information aids an attacker in identifying exploitable components and their versions. Demo record 5.
title: Verbose logging exposes internal stack traces to end users - Demo 5

description: The policy download endpoint accepts a numeric document ID as a URL parameter without verifying ownership. Any authenticated user can iterate IDs and download confidential policy documents belonging to other customers. Demo record 6.
mitigation: Upgrade io.demo:fake-db-driver to 4.5.9 and implement resource-level authorization checks on every download request.
title: Insecure Direct Object Reference in policy document download - Demo 6

description: Responses from the insurance portal do not include the Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header. Clients connecting over HTTP are not automatically upgraded to HTTPS, enabling potential downgrade attacks on insecure networks. Demo record 7.
title: Missing HTTP Strict-Transport-Security header - Demo 7

description: The login flow in the test environment accepts an arbitrary returnUrl parameter and redirects the user post-authentication without validating the destination domain, enabling phishing via trusted-domain redirects. Demo record 8.
mitigation: Upgrade org.example:demo-http-client to 2.1.4 and implement an allowlist of valid redirect destinations.
title: Open Redirect via unvalidated returnUrl query parameter - Demo 8

description: The S3 bucket used by the orders service for storing order attachments is configured with ACL public-read and has server-side encryption disabled. Any unauthenticated internet user can list and download customer order files. Demo record 9.
mitigation: Enable SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS encryption. Remove public-read ACL and enforce bucket policy to deny public access. Upgrade cache lib to 3.3.0.
title: AWS S3 bucket with public read access and no encryption at rest - Demo 9

description: An IAM role attached to the orders microservice grants "Action: *" on "Resource: *" without condition keys. A compromised instance can call any AWS API including IAM manipulation APIs, enabling full account takeover. Demo record 10.
mitigation: Restrict IAM role to least-privilege actions required by the service. Apply permission boundaries.
title: IAM role with wildcard action allows privilege escalation - Demo 10

description: AWS CloudTrail is not enabled for management event logging in the us-east-1 region. Privileged API calls including IAM changes and security group modifications go unrecorded, reducing visibility for incident response. Demo record 11.
title: CloudTrail logging disabled for management events in region us-east-1 - Demo 11

description: The EC2 security group associated with the insurance service allows inbound TCP port 22 (SSH) from all IPv4 addresses. This unnecessarily expands the attack surface and enables brute-force login attempts from the internet. Demo record 12.
mitigation: Restrict SSH access to a bastion host IP range or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to eliminate SSH exposure entirely.
title: Security group allows unrestricted inbound SSH access from 0.0.0.0/0 - Demo 12

description: The RDS database instance for the insurance service has automated backups disabled. A failure or ransomware event would result in irreversible data loss without a point-in-time recovery option. Demo record 13.
mitigation: Enable automated backups with a retention period of at least 7 days. Upgrade template engine to 6.3.2.
title: RDS instance with automated backups disabled - Demo 13

description: org.example:demo-http-client versions below 2.1.4 do not validate redirect destinations when following 3xx responses. An attacker controlling a server can redirect the client to internal network endpoints (e.g. AWS metadata service at 169.254.169.254). Demo record 14.
mitigation: Upgrade org.example:demo-http-client to 2.1.4.
title: HTTP Client: Server-Side Request Forgery via malformed redirect handling - Demo 14

description: com.example:fake-json-parser versions before 3.0.3 do not impose limits on object nesting depth. A crafted JSON payload with thousands of nested objects causes unbounded memory allocation and stack overflow, leading to denial of service. Demo record 15.
mitigation: Upgrade com.example:fake-json-parser to 3.0.3.
title: JSON Parser: Uncontrolled resource consumption via deeply nested input - Demo 15

description: com.demo:fake-crypto-lib prior to 1.2.5 uses a PRNG with a measurable statistical bias when generating RSA prime candidates. Sufficiently large samples of public keys generated by the affected library can be factored using a lattice-based attack. Demo record 16.
mitigation: Upgrade com.demo:fake-crypto-lib to 1.2.5.
title: Crypto Lib: Weak RSA key generation due to biased PRNG - Demo 16

description: io.demo:fake-db-driver versions before 4.5.9 deserialize server-provided responses without type validation. A malicious or compromised database server can deliver a gadget chain payload that executes arbitrary code on the application host. Demo record 17.
mitigation: Upgrade io.demo:fake-db-driver to 4.5.9 immediately.
title: DB Driver: Remote code execution via deserialization of untrusted data - Demo 17

description: io.example:demo-auth-lib before 5.1.3 accepts JWTs with the "none" algorithm if the header algorithm field is set to "None" (mixed case). An attacker can forge arbitrary JWT payloads and bypass authentication entirely. Demo record 18.
mitigation: Upgrade io.example:demo-auth-lib to 5.1.3 and explicitly reject the "none" algorithm in the JWT validation configuration.
title: Auth Lib: JWT signature verification bypass with algorithm confusion - Demo 18

description: com.example:demo-scheduler version 2.0.0 does not validate cron expressions before parsing. A user with job scheduling permissions can submit an expression with a zero-length time unit that causes an infinite loop in the scheduler thread. Demo record 19.
mitigation: Upgrade com.example:demo-scheduler to 2.0.2.
title: Scheduler: Denial of service via malformed cron expression - Demo 19

description: org.demo:fake-xml-parser versions before 1.1.0 do not set limits on XML entity expansion. A small XML document that references deeply nested entities can expand to gigabytes in memory, causing out-of-memory conditions. Demo record 20.
mitigation: Upgrade org.demo:fake-xml-parser to 1.1.0.
title: XML Parser: Billion laughs XML entity expansion attack - Demo 20

description: io.demo:fake-template-engine 6.3.0 evaluates template expressions found in user input without sandboxing. An attacker can inject template directives that execute arbitrary server-side code or read sensitive files from the filesystem. Demo record 21.
mitigation: Upgrade io.demo:fake-template-engine to 6.3.2 and always sanitize input before passing it to the template rendering context.
title: Template Engine: Server-side template injection via user-controlled input - Demo 21

description: The Docker image for the accounts service runs processes as root (UID 0) and mounts the application volume as read-write. A container escape or RCE vulnerability would immediately grant root-level access to the host namespace. Demo record 22.
mitigation: Define a non-root USER in the Dockerfile and set the root filesystem to read-only using securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem: true.
title: Container image running as root user without read-only filesystem - Demo 22

description: The orders service container image is built on a base OS image that reached end-of-life and no longer receives security patches. Several high-severity kernel and libc vulnerabilities remain unpatched in the base layer. Demo record 23.
mitigation: Rebuild image using a maintained base image (e.g. alpine:3.19 or debian:bookworm-slim). Pin base image digest to prevent supply-chain substitution.
title: Container image based on deprecated base OS with unpatched CVEs - Demo 23

description: Database credentials and API keys are injected as environment variables in the Kubernetes pod specification using plain-text values. Environment variables are readable by any process in the container and may be leaked via /proc inspection. Demo record 24.
title: Secrets mounted as environment variables in container spec - Demo 24

description: The insurance service Dockerfile does not define a HEALTHCHECK instruction. Without a health check, orchestrators cannot detect hung or unresponsive containers and may route traffic to degraded instances. Demo record 25.
mitigation: Add HEALTHCHECK INTERVAL=30s TIMEOUT=5s CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/actuator/health || exit 1 to the Dockerfile.
title: Container image has no defined HEALTHCHECK instruction - Demo 25

description: The Terraform remote state backend S3 bucket is configured without server-side encryption and without access logging. The state file contains sensitive infrastructure metadata including IAM role ARNs and database connection strings. Demo record 26.
mitigation: Enable SSE-KMS on the Terraform state bucket and configure S3 access logging to a separate audit bucket.
title: Terraform: S3 backend state file has no encryption or access logging - Demo 26

description: A ClusterRole definition in the Helm chart grants ["*"] verbs on core API resources including pods, secrets, and configmaps. Any service account bound to this role can read cluster-wide secrets and execute arbitrary commands in any pod. Demo record 27.
mitigation: Replace wildcard verbs with explicit minimal verb sets. Separate read and write roles. Upgrade scheduler to 2.0.2 to patch related scheduling flaw.
title: Kubernetes RBAC: ClusterRole binds wildcard verbs to core API group - Demo 27

description: Several Terraform modules declare providers without pinning to a specific version range. Uncontrolled provider upgrades during terraform init can introduce breaking changes or subtle behavioral differences in infrastructure configuration. Demo record 28.
title: Terraform module lacks required_providers version constraints - Demo 28

description: A transitive dependency of the orders service pulls in org.fake:demo-serializer 0.9.1, which appears on the internal security blacklist following the discovery of obfuscated exfiltration code in a previous patch release. Demo record 29.
mitigation: Remove org.fake:demo-serializer from the dependency graph. Replace with a vetted serialization alternative and verify no other transitive paths re-introduce the package.
title: Blacklisted dependency: known malicious package in transitive graph - Demo 29

mitigation: Upgrade com.fake:demo-session-manager to 3.0.0 or replace token generation with java.security.SecureRandom with at least 128-bit entropy.
title: Broken Authentication: Session tokens with insufficient entropy - Demo 30

description: The insurance service Dockerfile does not define a HEALTHCHECK instruction. Without a health check, orchestrators cannot detect hung or unresponsive containers and may route traffic to degraded instances. Demo record 83.
mitigation: Add HEALTHCHECK INTERVAL=30s TIMEOUT=5s CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/actuator/health || exit 1 to the Dockerfile.
title: Container image has no defined HEALTHCHECK instruction - Demo 83

description: The Terraform remote state backend S3 bucket is configured without server-side encryption and without access logging. The state file contains sensitive infrastructure metadata including IAM role ARNs and database connection strings. Demo record 84.
mitigation: Enable SSE-KMS on the Terraform state bucket and configure S3 access logging to a separate audit bucket.
title: Terraform: S3 backend state file has no encryption or access logging - Demo 84

description: A ClusterRole definition in the Helm chart grants ["*"] verbs on core API resources including pods, secrets, and configmaps. Any service account bound to this role can read cluster-wide secrets and execute arbitrary commands in any pod. Demo record 85.
mitigation: Replace wildcard verbs with explicit minimal verb sets. Separate read and write roles. Upgrade scheduler to 2.0.2 to patch related scheduling flaw.
title: Kubernetes RBAC: ClusterRole binds wildcard verbs to core API group - Demo 85

description: Several Terraform modules declare providers without pinning to a specific version range. Uncontrolled provider upgrades during terraform init can introduce breaking changes or subtle behavioral differences in infrastructure configuration. Demo record 86.
title: Terraform module lacks required_providers version constraints - Demo 86

description: A transitive dependency of the orders service pulls in org.fake:demo-serializer 0.9.1, which appears on the internal security blacklist following the discovery of obfuscated exfiltration code in a previous patch release. Demo record 87.
mitigation: Remove org.fake:demo-serializer from the dependency graph. Replace with a vetted serialization alternative and verify no other transitive paths re-introduce the package.
title: Blacklisted dependency: known malicious package in transitive graph - Demo 87

description: The session management module generates tokens with only 32 bits of entropy using a predictable PRNG seeded with system time. An attacker can brute-force valid tokens and hijack authenticated sessions without credentials. Demo record 88.
mitigation: Upgrade com.fake:demo-session-manager to 3.0.0 or replace token generation with java.security.SecureRandom with at least 128-bit entropy.
title: Broken Authentication: Session tokens with insufficient entropy - Demo 88

description: The user search endpoint concatenates user-supplied input directly into SQL statements without sanitization. An unauthenticated attacker can extract the full database contents or execute arbitrary DDL commands. Demo record 89.
mitigation: Upgrade io.example:demo-auth-lib to 5.1.3 and replace all dynamic query construction with prepared statements.
title: SQL Injection via unparameterized dynamic query builder - Demo 89

description: AES-128 encryption keys are hardcoded in the application source code and committed to the version control history. Any developer or CI/CD agent with repository access can retrieve and misuse these keys. Demo record 90.
mitigation: Rotate all exposed keys immediately. Move secrets to a vault solution (e.g. HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager). Remove key material from git history.
title: Sensitive Data Exposure: Cryptographic keys stored in source code - Demo 90

description: Error messages rendered by the XML parser module reflect unescaped request parameters into the HTML response. A remote attacker can craft a malicious link and execute arbitrary JavaScript in the victim's browser context. Demo record 91.
mitigation: Upgrade org.demo:fake-xml-parser to 1.1.0 and apply output encoding on all user-supplied values before HTML rendering.
title: Cross-Site Scripting (Reflected) in error response body - Demo 91

description: The logger library is configured to write full exception stack traces and internal class paths into HTTP responses in production. This information aids an attacker in identifying exploitable components and their versions. Demo record 92.
title: Verbose logging exposes internal stack traces to end users - Demo 92

description: The policy download endpoint accepts a numeric document ID as a URL parameter without verifying ownership. Any authenticated user can iterate IDs and download confidential policy documents belonging to other customers. Demo record 93.
mitigation: Upgrade io.demo:fake-db-driver to 4.5.9 and implement resource-level authorization checks on every download request.
title: Insecure Direct Object Reference in policy document download - Demo 93

description: Responses from the insurance portal do not include the Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) header. Clients connecting over HTTP are not automatically upgraded to HTTPS, enabling potential downgrade attacks on insecure networks. Demo record 94.
title: Missing HTTP Strict-Transport-Security header - Demo 94

description: The login flow in the test environment accepts an arbitrary returnUrl parameter and redirects the user post-authentication without validating the destination domain, enabling phishing via trusted-domain redirects. Demo record 95.
mitigation: Upgrade org.example:demo-http-client to 2.1.4 and implement an allowlist of valid redirect destinations.
title: Open Redirect via unvalidated returnUrl query parameter - Demo 95

description: The S3 bucket used by the orders service for storing order attachments is configured with ACL public-read and has server-side encryption disabled. Any unauthenticated internet user can list and download customer order files. Demo record 96.
mitigation: Enable SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS encryption. Remove public-read ACL and enforce bucket policy to deny public access. Upgrade cache lib to 3.3.0.
title: AWS S3 bucket with public read access and no encryption at rest - Demo 96

description: An IAM role attached to the orders microservice grants "Action: *" on "Resource: *" without condition keys. A compromised instance can call any AWS API including IAM manipulation APIs, enabling full account takeover. Demo record 97.
mitigation: Restrict IAM role to least-privilege actions required by the service. Apply permission boundaries.
title: IAM role with wildcard action allows privilege escalation - Demo 97

description: AWS CloudTrail is not enabled for management event logging in the us-east-1 region. Privileged API calls including IAM changes and security group modifications go unrecorded, reducing visibility for incident response. Demo record 98.
title: CloudTrail logging disabled for management events in region us-east-1 - Demo 98

description: The EC2 security group associated with the insurance service allows inbound TCP port 22 (SSH) from all IPv4 addresses. This unnecessarily expands the attack surface and enables brute-force login attempts from the internet. Demo record 99.
mitigation: Restrict SSH access to a bastion host IP range or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to eliminate SSH exposure entirely.
title: Security group allows unrestricted inbound SSH access from 0.0.0.0/0 - Demo 99

description: The RDS database instance for the insurance service has automated backups disabled. A failure or ransomware event would result in irreversible data loss without a point-in-time recovery option. Demo record 100.
mitigation: Enable automated backups with a retention period of at least 7 days. Upgrade template engine to 6.3.2.
title: RDS instance with automated backups disabled - Demo 100

description: org.example:demo-http-client versions below 2.1.4 do not validate redirect destinations when following 3xx responses. An attacker controlling a server can redirect the client to internal network endpoints (e.g. AWS metadata service at 169.254.169.254). Demo record 101.
mitigation: Upgrade org.example:demo-http-client to 2.1.4.
title: HTTP Client: Server-Side Request Forgery via malformed redirect handling - Demo 101

description: com.example:fake-json-parser versions before 3.0.3 do not impose limits on object nesting depth. A crafted JSON payload with thousands of nested objects causes unbounded memory allocation and stack overflow, leading to denial of service. Demo record 102.
mitigation: Upgrade com.example:fake-json-parser to 3.0.3.
title: JSON Parser: Uncontrolled resource consumption via deeply nested input - Demo 102

description: com.demo:fake-crypto-lib prior to 1.2.5 uses a PRNG with a measurable statistical bias when generating RSA prime candidates. Sufficiently large samples of public keys generated by the affected library can be factored using a lattice-based attack. Demo record 103.
mitigation: Upgrade com.demo:fake-crypto-lib to 1.2.5.
title: Crypto Lib: Weak RSA key generation due to biased PRNG - Demo 103

description: io.demo:fake-db-driver versions before 4.5.9 deserialize server-provided responses without type validation. A malicious or compromised database server can deliver a gadget chain payload that executes arbitrary code on the application host. Demo record 104.
mitigation: Upgrade io.demo:fake-db-driver to 4.5.9 immediately.
title: DB Driver: Remote code execution via deserialization of untrusted data - Demo 104

description: io.example:demo-auth-lib before 5.1.3 accepts JWTs with the "none" algorithm if the header algorithm field is set to "None" (mixed case). An attacker can forge arbitrary JWT payloads and bypass authentication entirely. Demo record 105.
mitigation: Upgrade io.example:demo-auth-lib to 5.1.3 and explicitly reject the "none" algorithm in the JWT validation configuration.
title: Auth Lib: JWT signature verification bypass with algorithm confusion - Demo 105

description: com.example:demo-scheduler version 2.0.0 does not validate cron expressions before parsing. A user with job scheduling permissions can submit an expression with a zero-length time unit that causes an infinite loop in the scheduler thread. Demo record 106.
mitigation: Upgrade com.example:demo-scheduler to 2.0.2.
title: Scheduler: Denial of service via malformed cron expression - Demo 106

description: org.demo:fake-xml-parser versions before 1.1.0 do not set limits on XML entity expansion. A small XML document that references deeply nested entities can expand to gigabytes in memory, causing out-of-memory conditions. Demo record 107.
mitigation: Upgrade org.demo:fake-xml-parser to 1.1.0.
title: XML Parser: Billion laughs XML entity expansion attack - Demo 107

description: io.demo:fake-template-engine 6.3.0 evaluates template expressions found in user input without sandboxing. An attacker can inject template directives that execute arbitrary server-side code or read sensitive files from the filesystem. Demo record 108.
mitigation: Upgrade io.demo:fake-template-engine to 6.3.2 and always sanitize input before passing it to the template rendering context.
title: Template Engine: Server-side template injection via user-controlled input - Demo 108

description: The Docker image for the accounts service runs processes as root (UID 0) and mounts the application volume as read-write. A container escape or RCE vulnerability would immediately grant root-level access to the host namespace. Demo record 109.
mitigation: Define a non-root USER in the Dockerfile and set the root filesystem to read-only using securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem: true.
title: Container image running as root user without read-only filesystem - Demo 109

description: The orders service container image is built on a base OS image that reached end-of-life and no longer receives security patches. Several high-severity kernel and libc vulnerabilities remain unpatched in the base layer. Demo record 110.
mitigation: Rebuild image using a maintained base image (e.g. alpine:3.19 or debian:bookworm-slim). Pin base image digest to prevent supply-chain substitution.
title: Container image based on deprecated base OS with unpatched CVEs - Demo 110
